summary: TechCrunch reported ClickUp laid off 22% of its workforce after CEO Zeb Evans announced a 100x organization push built around internal AI agents and AI-enabled productivity gains.
evidenceQuote: had laid off 22% of its workforce ... a radical embrace of AI
notes: TechCrunch reported Evans characterized the 22% workforce reduction as an AI-driven operating shift rather than a cost-cutting move. The article also says ClickUp recently introduced roughly 3,000 internal AI agents and expects employees to direct and review those agents' work. Because AI agents and AI-enabled productivity are explicitly cited as the restructuring rationale, this is classified as explicit AI cited. No absolute layoff count is used because the source gives only a percentage.

sources.name: TechCrunch
sources.quote: had laid off 22% of its workforce ... a radical embrace of AI
